warriors.subject: Chhatrapati Shivaji Maharaj
warriors.text: Shivaji Maharaj was one of the greatest kings India has ever seen. He was born in 1630 in a place called Pune in Maharashtra. His mother, Jija Bai, was a very wise and strong woman who told him stories of brave heroes every night. She taught Shivaji to always protect good people and stand up against what is wrong. When Shivaji grew up, he saw that the Mughal Emperor Aurangzeb was troubling the people of his land. So Shivaji decided to build his own army and fight back. He used very clever fighting tricks called guerrilla warfare, where his small army would hide in the jungles and hills and surprise the big Mughal army. He captured many forts and made them very strong. His most famous fort was Raigad, and it was there that he was crowned Chhatrapati, which means the great king, in the year 1674. People loved him because he was fair, kind to all religions, and always kept his people safe. Even today, children in Maharashtra grow up hearing his amazing stories.

questions.question: Who did Shivaji Maharaj fight against?
What is Shivaji's mother's name?
What type of fighting did Shivaji use?
Where was Shivaji crowned as king?

warriors.subject: Maharana Pratap
warriors.text: Maharana Pratap was a mighty Rajput king who ruled a kingdom called Mewar in Rajasthan. He was born in 1540 and grew up learning to ride horses, use swords, and protect his people. When the powerful Mughal Emperor Akbar wanted all kings to accept his rule, most kings said yes. But not Maharana Pratap. He proudly said no and refused to bow down. In 1576, he fought a very famous battle called the Battle of Haldighati against Akbar's general, Man Singh. Even though Pratap's army was smaller, he fought with great courage. His beloved horse Chetak was so loyal that even when he was badly hurt, he carried Pratap to safety before falling down. After the battle, Pratap lived in the forests with his family. They ate very simple food and slept under the open sky. But Pratap never gave up. He slowly took back his lands and kept fighting till his last breath in 1597. He is remembered forever as a king who never surrendered and always loved his freedom.

questions.question: Which Mughal emperor did Maharana Pratap refuse to obey?
What was the name of Maharana Pratap's loyal horse?
What was the famous battle Maharana Pratap fought?
Did Maharana Pratap ever give up to the Mughals?

warriors.subject: Sambhaji Maharaj
warriors.text: Sambhaji Maharaj was the fearless son of the great Chhatrapati Shivaji Maharaj. He was born in 1657 and was a very clever boy from a young age. He learned to read and write many languages, and he even wrote books and poems when he was still young. After his father passed away, Sambhaji became the king of the Maratha Empire. He continued to fight the powerful Mughal Emperor Aurangzeb just like his father had done. Aurangzeb had a very big army and wanted to destroy the Maratha kingdom. But Sambhaji fought bravely for many years and never let Aurangzeb win easily. Sadly, one day Sambhaji was captured by Aurangzeb's soldiers. Aurangzeb asked him to leave his religion and accept Islam, but Sambhaji bravely refused. Even when Aurangzeb was very cruel and harsh to him, Sambhaji kept smiling and said he would never give up his faith or his people. He gave his life, but he never surrendered. The people of Maharashtra remember him as Dharamveer, which means a hero who died for his faith. He is a symbol of bravery, loyalty, and love for one's land.

questions.question: Who was Sambhaji Maharaj's father?
What did Aurangzeb ask Sambhaji to do?
What is Sambhaji called by the people of Maharashtra?
What special talent did Sambhaji have besides fighting?

warriors.subject: Prithviraj Chauhan
warriors.text: Prithviraj Chauhan was a great and noble Rajput king who ruled Delhi and Ajmer a very long time ago, around the year 1168. He was known all over the land for being a brave warrior and a kind-hearted king. Even as a young boy, he was very talented. It is said that he could shoot an arrow just by hearing the sound of a target, without even looking at it. This special skill was called shabdbhedi baan. When a foreign invader named Muhammad of Ghor came with a big army to attack India, Prithviraj fought him bravely and defeated him in the famous First Battle of Tarain in 1191. He even captured Muhammad of Ghor but let him go, because Prithviraj was generous and believed in giving second chances. Sadly, Muhammad of Ghor came back again with an even bigger army. This time, Prithviraj was defeated and captured. Even in captivity, Prithviraj showed his amazing skill. His court poet Chand Bardai helped him use his shabdbhedi skill one last time to face his enemy bravely. Prithviraj Chauhan is remembered as the last Hindu king of Delhi and a true hero of India.

questions.question: What was Prithviraj Chauhan's special skill with a bow and arrow?
Who did Prithviraj Chauhan fight against?
What did Prithviraj do after capturing Muhammad of Ghor the first time?
What is Prithviraj Chauhan remembered as?

warriors.subject: Rani Lakshmibai of Jhansi
warriors.text: Rani Lakshmibai was one of the bravest queens India has ever had. She was born in 1828 in a city called Varanasi and her childhood name was Manikarnika, but everyone lovingly called her Manu. She learned horse riding, sword fighting, and archery when she was very young, which was very unusual for girls at that time. She married the King of Jhansi and became the queen. When the British rulers tried to take over her kingdom by force, she refused to give it up. She famously said she would never give her Jhansi to the British. In 1857, a big revolt happened against British rule all over India. Rani Lakshmibai fought courageously in this revolt. She rode on her horse with her young son tied to her back so that both her hands were free to fight. She fought so bravely that even the British soldiers who fought against her praised her courage. She gave her life in the battle but never surrendered. She is remembered forever as the warrior queen who inspired millions of Indians to fight for freedom.

questions.question: What was Rani Lakshmibai's childhood name?
Which kingdom did Rani Lakshmibai rule?
Who did Rani Lakshmibai fight against?
What did Rani Lakshmibai do with her son during the battle?
options: Tied him to her back while fighting
questions.correct: Tied him to her back while fighting

warriors.subject: Bhagat Singh
warriors.text: Bhagat Singh was a young and fearless freedom fighter who loved his country more than his own life. He was born in 1907 in Punjab, which is now partly in Pakistan. From a very young age, he saw British soldiers being unfair and cruel to Indian people. This made him very angry and he decided to fight for India's freedom. He read many books and became very smart and thoughtful. When a brave Indian leader named Lala Lajpat Rai was hurt badly by the British police, Bhagat Singh could not stay quiet. He and his friends took action to show the world that Indians would not stay silent. He also threw pamphlets inside the British parliament to make his voice heard, because he wanted the whole world to know that India deserved freedom. He was arrested by the British and put in jail. Even in jail, he was calm and cheerful. He was only 23 years old when the British hanged him in 1931. He smiled and sang songs of freedom till the very end. He is called Shaheed-e-Azam, which means the greatest martyr, and is a hero to every Indian child even today.

questions.question: What is Bhagat Singh called by Indians?
How old was Bhagat Singh when he was hanged?
Which Indian leader's injury inspired Bhagat Singh to take action?
Where did Bhagat Singh throw pamphlets to make his voice heard?

warriors.subject: Guru Gobind Singh
warriors.text: Guru Gobind Singh was the tenth and last human Guru of the Sikh religion. He was born in 1666 in Patna, Bihar. He was a great warrior, a wise poet, and a wonderful teacher all at the same time. He grew up seeing the Mughal Emperor Aurangzeb being very cruel to Hindus and Sikhs. When Guru Gobind Singh was just nine years old, his father Guru Tegh Bahadur gave his life to protect the freedom of Hindus to practise their religion. This made young Gobind very strong in his heart. When he grew up, he created a special group of brave warriors called the Khalsa in 1699. He asked five fearless volunteers to come forward and they became the first Panj Pyare, meaning the five beloved ones. He taught them never to bow to injustice. He fought many battles against the Mughal army and the hill kings who worked with Mughals. Even when he lost his four young sons, he never gave up. He said that where there is righteousness, there is victory. His courage and teachings continue to inspire Sikhs and Indians all over the world even today.

questions.question: Which Guru was Guru Gobind Singh?
What special group of warriors did Guru Gobind Singh create?
What are the first five members of the Khalsa called?
Who was Guru Gobind Singh's father?

warriors.subject: Chandra Shekhar Azad
warriors.text: Chandra Shekhar Azad was a daring and passionate freedom fighter who burned with love for his country. He was born in 1906 in Madhya Pradesh. From the time he was a teenager, he joined protests against British rule. When the British police caught him once and asked his name, he proudly said his name was Azad, which means free. He said his father's name was Independence and his home was jail. The British punished him by whipping him, but Azad did not cry. He only chanted Vande Mataram, meaning I bow to my motherland. That is how he got the name Azad. He was a close friend and teammate of Bhagat Singh. Together they worked bravely to fight British injustice. Azad was an expert at handling guns and he trained many young freedom fighters. He always carried a pistol and had made a promise to himself that he would never be caught alive by the British. On 27 February 1931, when British police surrounded him at Alfred Park in Allahabad, he fought back bravely. True to his word, he never surrendered. He is forever remembered as a free man who lived free and died free.

questions.question: What does the name Azad mean?
What did Azad chant when the British whipped him?
Who was Chandra Shekhar Azad's close friend and fellow freedom fighter?
What promise did Azad make to himself?
options: To never be caught alive by the British
To become the president of India
questions.correct: To never be caught alive by the British

warriors.subject: Subhas Chandra Bose
warriors.text: Subhas Chandra Bose, lovingly called Netaji, was a strong and determined freedom fighter who wanted India to be free from British rule at any cost. He was born in 1897 in Cuttack, Odisha, and was very brilliant in his studies. He even passed the very tough Indian Civil Services exam, but he left the job because he did not want to work for the British. He believed that Indians needed to fight boldly for their freedom and not just ask politely. He started an army called the Indian National Army, also known as the Azad Hind Fauj. This army had brave soldiers, and even many women joined it under the leadership of Captain Lakshmi Sahgal. Netaji gave a famous call that said, Give me blood and I will give you freedom. His voice inspired millions of young Indians. He travelled to Germany and Japan to get help for India's freedom struggle. The Azad Hind Fauj fought against the British in northeast India. Netaji is remembered as a fierce, fearless leader who never gave up on his dream of a free India. His memory still fills Indian hearts with pride and passion.

questions.question: What is the loving name people call Subhas Chandra Bose?
What was the name of the army Netaji started?
What was Netaji's famous call to Indians?
options: Give me blood and I will give you freedom
questions.correct: Give me blood and I will give you freedom

questions.question: Which countries did Netaji travel to for help?

warriors.subject: Tanaji Malusare
warriors.text: Tanaji Malusare was one of the most loyal and bravest commanders in Shivaji Maharaj's army. He was like a best friend and a trusted soldier to Shivaji. One of the most famous stories about Tanaji is the battle of Sinhagad fort, which happened in 1670. The Sinhagad fort, which was earlier called Kondhana, was a very strong fort held by the Mughals. Shivaji wanted to take it back. Tanaji bravely said he would do it. Even though his son's wedding was coming up, Tanaji said the wedding of the fort was more important and left for the battle. He and his brave soldiers climbed the steep fort walls at night using a large monitor lizard named Yashwanti, who held a rope so the soldiers could climb up. They surprised the Mughal soldiers in the dark. Tanaji fought with great bravery and courage. He won the fort for Shivaji, but sadly gave his life in the battle. When Shivaji heard the news, he was very sad and said, The fort is won, but the lion is gone. Tanaji is remembered forever as the lion of Shivaji's army.

questions.question: What was the earlier name of Sinhagad fort?
What did Tanaji use to help his soldiers climb the fort walls?
options: A rope tied to a monitor lizard
questions.correct: A rope tied to a monitor lizard

questions.question: What did Shivaji say when he heard that Tanaji had died?
options: The fort is won, but the lion is gone
questions.correct: The fort is won, but the lion is gone

questions.question: What was happening in Tanaji's family when he left for battle?

warriors.subject: Rani Durgavati
warriors.text: Rani Durgavati was a brave and wise queen who ruled the Gondwana kingdom in central India. She was born in 1524 in a Rajput family and was trained in horse riding, sword fighting, and archery just like a warrior. She married the Gond king Dalpat Shah and ruled the kingdom after he passed away, with great skill and care. Her kingdom was peaceful and the people were happy. But then the Mughal Emperor Akbar sent his general Asaf Khan with a big army to take over her kingdom. Rani Durgavati did not run away or give up. She put on her armour and rode her favourite elephant into battle herself. She fought two big battles against the Mughal army with tremendous bravery. Her soldiers were very inspired seeing their queen fight alongside them. Even when she was hurt by arrows in battle, she kept fighting. When she realised she could not win, she chose death over surrender. She pierced herself with her own dagger so that the enemy could never capture her. She is remembered as Shaheed Rani, the martyr queen, and her bravery lives on in the hearts of people in Madhya Pradesh even today.

questions.question: Which kingdom did Rani Durgavati rule?
Which Mughal emperor sent his army against Rani Durgavati?
What did Rani Durgavati ride into battle?
What did Rani Durgavati do instead of surrendering to the enemy?
options: She ran away to the forest
She asked for help from another king

warriors.subject: Lachit Borphukan
warriors.text: Lachit Borphukan was a great warrior hero from Assam in northeast India. He was born around 1622 and served as a top commander for the Ahom kingdom. The Ahom kings had ruled Assam for hundreds of years and protected it from many invaders. When the mighty Mughal Emperor Aurangzeb sent a huge army with thousands of soldiers and big war boats to take over Assam, it was Lachit Borphukan who stood up to protect his land. The most famous battle he fought was the Battle of Saraighat in 1671, which was fought on the Brahmaputra river. The Mughals had more soldiers and bigger boats, but Lachit had cleverness and love for his homeland. He came up with a brilliant plan to use small fast boats to attack the big Mughal ships. There is a famous story that during the battle, Lachit was very sick with a high fever. His soldiers were tired and started to slow down. But Lachit rose from his sick bed, got into a small boat himself, and led the charge against the Mughals. His bravery inspired all his soldiers and they won a great victory. He is the greatest hero of Assam and every year on 24 November, Assam celebrates Lachit Divas in his honour.

questions.question: Which kingdom did Lachit Borphukan protect?
What was the famous battle Lachit fought on the Brahmaputra river?
What condition was Lachit in during the Battle of Saraighat?
When is Lachit Divas celebrated in Assam?

warriors.subject: Banda Singh Bahadur
warriors.text: Banda Singh Bahadur was a brave Sikh warrior who fought fearlessly against the cruel Mughal rulers who were hurting innocent people. He was born in 1670 in Rajouri. Earlier in his life he was a holy man who lived in a forest. But when Guru Gobind Singh met him and told him about the suffering of innocent people at the hands of Mughal governor Wazir Khan, Banda Singh was moved deeply. Guru Gobind Singh gave him five arrows and his blessings and asked him to go and fight for justice. Banda Singh obeyed and became a mighty warrior commander. He built a brave army of Sikh warriors and won many battles. His greatest achievement was defeating Wazir Khan, who had killed Guru Gobind Singh's two young sons, in the Battle of Chappar Chiri in 1710. After winning, he established a small Sikh rule in Punjab, which was a proud moment for all Sikhs. He gave land to poor farmers and treated all people fairly. Sadly, the Mughals captured him after some years. Even in captivity and facing great cruelty, Banda Singh never gave up his faith. He is remembered as a mighty warrior who fought for justice, equality, and the protection of innocent people.

questions.question: Who inspired Banda Singh Bahadur to become a warrior?
Which cruel Mughal governor did Banda Singh defeat?
What did Guru Gobind Singh give Banda Singh before sending him to fight?
What did Banda Singh do for poor farmers after winning?

warriors.subject: Velu Nachiyar
warriors.text: Velu Nachiyar was a remarkable and extraordinary queen from Tamil Nadu in South India. She was born in 1730 and was the queen of Sivaganga. She is one of the first Indian queens to have fought against British rule, even before the famous revolt of 1857. What makes her really special is that she trained in sword fighting, horse riding, and even in martial arts. She could speak many languages including French and English. When the British and their ally the Nawab of Arcot attacked her kingdom and killed her husband, she escaped with her young daughter. She did not give up. She spent years planning and building her strength. She formed an alliance with the great Hyder Ali of Mysore to fight the British. One of the most amazing stories about her is about her brave soldier Kuyili, who sacrificed her life in a special mission to destroy the British ammunition storage. Velu Nachiyar also created one of the world's first known human bomb missions in history. She finally defeated the British and took back her kingdom. She ruled wisely for many more years. The people of Tamil Nadu call her Veeramangai, which means brave woman, and she is one of the most inspiring queens in all of Indian history.

questions.question: What is Velu Nachiyar called by the people of Tamil Nadu?
Who did Velu Nachiyar ally with to fight the British?
What did her brave soldier Kuyili sacrifice her life to destroy?
From which state in India was Velu Nachiyar?

warriors.subject: Bal Gangadhar Tilak
warriors.text: Bal Gangadhar Tilak was a great leader, scholar, and freedom fighter who is called the Father of the Indian Independence Movement. He was born in 1856 in Maharashtra. He was very brilliant in mathematics and law. Tilak believed deeply that every Indian deserved to be free and that it was every Indian's birthright. His famous saying was, Swarajya is my birthright and I shall have it. Swarajya means self-rule or freedom. He used two very clever ideas to unite Indians. The first was to celebrate Ganesh Chaturthi as a big public festival so that people from all communities could come together. The second was to start Shivaji Jayanti celebrations to remind people of the great Maratha warrior king. The British were very afraid of his powerful words. They put him in jail many times. Even in jail, he kept writing and produced a famous book called Gita Rahasya. He started two important newspapers called Kesari and Mahratta to spread the message of freedom. He inspired millions of ordinary Indians to stand up against British rule. Tilak passed away in 1920, but his words and ideas kept the freedom movement alive for many more years.

questions.question: What is Bal Gangadhar Tilak's famous saying about freedom?
options: Give me blood and I will give you freedom
Swarajya is my birthright and I shall have it
questions.correct: Swarajya is my birthright and I shall have it

questions.question: Which festival did Tilak use to bring Indians together?
What book did Tilak write in jail?
What does the word Swarajya mean?

warriors.subject: Kittur Rani Chennamma
warriors.text: Kittur Rani Chennamma was a fearless queen from Karnataka who fought against British rule even before most people had started thinking about freedom. She was born in 1778 in a small village called Kakati. She learned horse riding, sword fighting, and archery from a very young age. She married the king of Kittur, which was a small but proud kingdom in Karnataka. When her husband and son both passed away, she became the queen and ruled bravely. The British had a policy called Doctrine of Lapse, which said if a king had no son, the British would take over the kingdom. Rani Chennamma said this was unfair and refused to give up Kittur. In 1824, when the British sent soldiers to take over Kittur, Rani Chennamma fought back with her army. Her soldiers were so brave and well-trained that they won the first battle and even captured two British officers. The whole of India was inspired. But the British came back with a much bigger army. Rani Chennamma fought with all her strength, but was eventually captured. She spent her remaining years in a British prison but never regretted fighting for her kingdom. She is remembered as a great heroine of Karnataka and an early symbol of India's fight for freedom.

questions.question: Which state in India was Kittur Rani Chennamma from?
What was the British policy that Rani Chennamma refused to accept?
What happened in the first battle Rani Chennamma fought against the British?
options: She won and captured two British officers
questions.correct: She won and captured two British officers

questions.question: How did Rani Chennamma spend her last years?

warriors.subject: Hemu Vikramaditya
warriors.text: Hemu Vikramaditya was a remarkable and inspiring man who rose from being a simple salt and grain trader to becoming one of the greatest Hindu kings and military commanders in medieval India. He was born around 1501 in a small town in Rajasthan. What is amazing about Hemu is that he was not from a royal family at all. He was an ordinary man who became extraordinary through his hard work, intelligence, and bravery. He won twenty-two battles in a row without losing a single one, which is a record that very few warriors in history can match. He served as a trusted general and prime minister for the Afghan Sultan Adil Shah. As Hemu kept winning battles, he grew more and more powerful. In 1556, he defeated the Mughal army and captured Delhi. He declared himself king of Delhi and took the royal title of Vikramaditya. This was a very proud moment because after many centuries, a Hindu king was sitting on the throne of Delhi. But soon the young Mughal Emperor Akbar's forces came back with a new general. At the Second Battle of Panipat, Hemu was winning when he was badly hurt by an arrow. He is remembered as a brave common man who almost changed the history of India.

questions.question: What was Hemu's profession before becoming a warrior?
How many battles did Hemu win in a row?
What royal title did Hemu give himself when he captured Delhi?
In which battle was Hemu defeated by the Mughals?

warriors.subject: Ahilyabai Holkar
warriors.text: Ahilyabai Holkar was one of the wisest and most beloved queens India has ever seen. She was born in 1725 in a small village in Maharashtra. What is wonderful about her story is that she was the daughter of a simple farmer. When a great Maratha chief named Malhar Rao Holkar saw her praying with great devotion at a temple, he was so impressed that he chose her to marry his son. Ahilyabai became a very skilled queen. After her husband and father-in-law passed away, she took over the kingdom of Malwa and ruled it with great wisdom. She was a brilliant administrator who took care of roads, rest houses for travellers, and water wells for the people. She built and restored hundreds of beautiful temples across India, including the famous Kashi Vishwanath Temple in Varanasi. She treated all people fairly, no matter how rich or poor they were. People used to come to her court and she would listen to everyone's problems patiently. Even though she faced great sadness in her personal life, she never stopped serving her people. She is called Lokamata, which means mother of the people. She is one of the most respected women in Indian history, remembered not just for bravery but for building and deeply caring for her kingdom.

questions.question: What does the title Lokamata mean?
Which famous temple did Ahilyabai Holkar rebuild?
Which kingdom did Ahilyabai Holkar rule?
What was Ahilyabai's father's profession?

warriors.subject: Tipu Sultan
warriors.text: Tipu Sultan was a powerful and innovative king of Mysore in South India who fought very bravely against the British. He was born in 1750 and was the son of Hyder Ali, another great warrior king. Tipu Sultan is often called the Tiger of Mysore because he was as fierce and brave as a tiger. He loved tigers so much that his throne, sword, and even his clothes had tiger patterns on them. Tipu Sultan was far ahead of his time. He developed one of the world's earliest rocket weapons, called Mysorean rockets, which were made of iron tubes and could travel long distances to hit enemy positions. These rockets frightened the British army greatly. He fought four wars against the British, known as the Anglo-Mysore Wars. In three of them, he either won or forced the British to make a peace treaty. He also wrote letters to foreign countries like France asking for help to fight the British together. Tipu Sultan never bowed to the British. In 1799, when the British attacked his capital Srirangapatna with a very big army, Tipu fought till the very last moment. He died defending the walls of his fort. He is remembered as a brave king who used modern ideas and technology to protect his land from the British.

questions.question: What is Tipu Sultan commonly called?
What special weapon did Tipu Sultan develop?
Who was Tipu Sultan's father?
Where did Tipu Sultan die defending his land?

warriors.subject: Mangal Pandey
warriors.text: Mangal Pandey was a brave Indian soldier who sparked the very first flame of the great revolt against British rule in 1857. He was born in 1827 in a small village in Uttar Pradesh and joined the British Indian Army as a young man. At that time, Indian soldiers in the British army were called sepoys. Mangal Pandey was a very proud and devoted Hindu soldier. In early 1857, there was a big rumour spreading among the Indian soldiers that the new rifle cartridges they had to use were greased with cow and pig fat. For Hindus, the cow is very sacred, and for Muslims, pigs are forbidden. The soldiers had to bite these cartridges open with their teeth, which deeply hurt the religious feelings of both Hindu and Muslim soldiers. Mangal Pandey was furious. On 29 March 1857, at the Barrackpore army base near Kolkata, he openly refused to follow British orders and attacked two British officers. He called upon all Indian soldiers to rise up against the British. His act of courage was like a spark that started a huge fire. Though he was caught and executed by the British on 8 April 1857, his bravery inspired a massive revolt that spread across India. He is remembered as the first hero of India's war of independence.

questions.question: What was the reason Indian soldiers were angry about the new rifle cartridges?
options: They were greased with cow and pig fat
questions.correct: They were greased with cow and pig fat

questions.question: Where did Mangal Pandey's revolt take place?
What were Indian soldiers in the British army called?
When was Mangal Pandey born?
